methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Manifold Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Gleb Kuznetsov and Pierce Ogden, having raised $40M to date. Valuation assessment draws on a universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, and Asimov.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $40M raised as a proxy for enterprise value, implying $80M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $100M (low) to $3.3B (high). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $68M to $931M, with a median of $445M. The substantial spread reflects material stage and traction dispersion within the peer set. Key uncertainties include clinical-stage advancement relative to Series B peers (Eikon, Neko Health valued at $3.3B and $1.8B respectively), capital efficiency relative to the $40M raised, and whether recent funding rounds signal momentum or valuation reset. Stage-progression anchors conservatively at $80M; peer-median dominates the range at $810M. The $445M median reflects weighted consideration of both methods.
